vitals.field_size: Up to 1,000 entries (250 Ultra, 500 Classic, 250 short)

climate.description: May in Yorkshire typically features mild spring conditions with variable weather patterns common to northern England.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 77. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 73.

course_description.character: Struggle Dales is a redesigned-for-2026, three-distance sportive (250 km Ultra, 165 km Classic, 120 km Short) based near Harrogate, running on paved roads through the Yorkshire Dales National Park's steep, rolling terrain. The 165 km Classic route contains six principal climbs; the 250 km Ultra contains ten. Sustained climbing rather than one isolated summit defines the route — one rider's 2026 GPS track logged 166.7 km and 3,116 m on the Classic, roughly 18.7 climbing metres per kilometre, though the organizer does not publish an official elevation figure.
course_description.signature_challenge: The organizer has not published individual climb gradients for the redesigned 2026 route — the difficulty comes from the accumulation of six (Classic) or ten (Ultra) named climbs rather than one standout ascent.
suffering_zones: Langbar — a new ascent added to the 2026 Classic route
High Hill — another new ascent added for 2026
Norwood Edge — also newly included in the redesigned route
The route overall — sustained, repeated climbing (roughly 18.7 m/km on one rider's recorded 2026 Classic ride) rather than a single defining summit

logistics.lodging_strategy: The start/finish is located just a few miles from Harrogate Town Centre, which hosts a wide range of hotels, bed and breakfasts, and guesthouses to suit all budgets. Camping and campervans are not permitted to stay overnight at the Army Foundation College venue.
logistics.transport: Struggle Dales begins and ends in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, a historic spa town offering excellent accommodation options and support infrastructure for participants and their crews.

history.origin_story: The 2026 edition is billed by the organizer as the tenth running of the event. Cycling Weekly reviewed an early edition (calling it a rising 'classic hardman ride' at the time), but the exact founding year is not independently confirmed — earlier file data claiming a 2010 founding conflicts with organizer materials describing the event as only in its second year in that same review period.

biased_opinion.verdict: Genuinely Tough, Well-Run — But Bring Your Own Elevation Data
biased_opinion.summary: Struggle Dales is a real, well-supported UK sportive marking its tenth anniversary in 2026, offering three distances (120/165/250 km) through the Yorkshire Dales with route-switching permitted on the day. Cycling Weekly reviewed an early edition favorably, and the organizer backs the event with marshals, medical/mechanical support, a hotline, broomwagon, signposted courses with GPX files, and instant results. The route was redesigned for 2026 with new named climbs (Langbar, High Hill, Norwood Edge), but the organizer publishes rounded distances only — no official elevation or detailed climb-gradient data for the current route, so riders are left triangulating difficulty from other entrants' GPS tracks rather than the organizer's own materials.
strengths: Three distances with permitted route switching on the day, plus signposted courses and downloadable GPX files
Solid event-day support: marshals, medical/mechanical assistance, hotline, broomwagon, stocked feeds
Free finish photographs, instant results, free parking, medal and bidon included
Reviewed favorably by Cycling Weekly in its early years, and now billed as the event's tenth edition in 2026
weaknesses: No official elevation figure published for the 2026 route — the 2,874 m previously on file matched the legacy course, not the redesigned 2026 Classic route
No running water or showers at the start/finish
Entries are non-refundable, with deadline-limited transfers and deferrals only
The organizer's own landing page has visibly retained placeholder testimonial cards — a small but real quality-control blemish for a marquee event
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A genuinely demanding, well-organized Yorkshire Dales sportive worth entering on reputation and support alone — just don't take the published distance/elevation figures as gospel until you've checked a recent GPS track from the current route.

final_verdict.one_liner: A genuinely tough, well-supported Yorkshire Dales sportive marking its tenth anniversary — the 2026 route was redesigned and the organizer still hasn't published official elevation for it.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want a legitimately hard, well-marshalled UK sportive with route-switching flexibility. Just verify current climb/elevation data from a recent rider's GPS track rather than trusting the organizer's rounded distance figures alone.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a similarly demanding Yorkshire Dales sportive, look at Étape du Dales or Struggle Moors (same organizer family) for a different route through the same region.

eligibility.notes: 2026 (tenth-anniversary) edition held Sunday 24 May 2026 per official listing, timing page and same-day rider activity. Distance corrected to 165 km Classic (down from the legacy 175.4 km course figure); elevation is a rider-recorded estimate (3,116 m), not an official organizer figure. Verified 2026-07-24 via codex gpt-5.6-sol foreground research as part of the Roadie Labs editorial wave 3.
